title: Community
description: Simple modpack for fun with friends! - Minecraft was meant to be played together; Adding mods just dials it up a notch!
body:
## Overview

This modpack allows players to enjoy modded skyblock while not having to wait and grind for several hours to get into the fun parts of modded Minecraft. It does this by easing the starting processes of several mods, making it fun for new players.

We designed this pack to be played with friends on a dedicated server, but you can play solo or on LAN as well.

It was meant to be played as a skyblock, but you can play it with normal terrain too!

## Object of Play

The goal in this modpack is to grind your way through the quests to eventually craft a full set of Infinity armor and tools/weapons. There is even 1 extra bonus quest for those who want to do that.

## Length of Play

This modpack should take advanced players 1 to 2 months to fully complete depending on how active they are and how much they already know about the mods included in the pack.

For players who are new to modded Minecraft or for players who are new to the mods inside this pack, it might take a little bit longer, but we aim to include a lot of good information inside the modpack to get players started (done via quests).

## Helpful Tips for Playing

Get yourself set up with a Refined Storage or Applied Energistics storage system as fast as possible and grind setting up auto crafting. There is going to be a lot of crafting you need to do in this modpack, so getting set up with a good way to automate this as fast as possible would help a lot.

Getting Mystical Aggriculture seeds set up as fast as possible will also allow you to get a quick start on needed materials. The quest book will guide you through which seeds you should craft first. Resource Essences (gotten from Mystical Aggriculture seeds) is probably one of the biggest parts of the modpack, so getting this automated will be very helpful.

Also, try to get to the nether as fast as possible so you can get your hands on copious amounts of nether quartz. The nether is default so you do not need to worry about not having resources and this is intended behavior to help progress the modpack.

And getting a backpack that has a feeding upgrade and having a hopper botany pot with carrots for golden carrots will go a long way to ensure peak efficiency.

## Mods

We focus mainly on the Tech, Magic, and Exploration mods in the modpack; but there are still other mods that we dive into.

#### Sample of Provided Tech Mods

*   Applied Energistics
*   Refined Storage
*   Mekanism
*   Ender IO
*   Thermal
*   Create
*   Powah!
*   Pipez

#### Sample of Provided Magic Mods

*   Botania
*   Elemental Craft

#### Other Notable Mods

*   (Fork of) Ex Nihilo
*   FTB Modset
    *   FTB Quests
    *   FTB Teams
    *   FTB Chunks
    *   FTB Essentials
    *   And more…
*   Botania: Garden of Glass

#### Performance is Key

We have also provided quite a few performance mods in this modpack.

## Why install Community?

The simplicity of the questline along with key mods like Refined Storage and Mekanism make this modpack great for both new and returning modded Minecraft players. And the performance improvements this pack provides allow you to enjoy it even more!

## Set Up

### Quick Note about the Server Pack downloads (SRV zip files)

Please note that when downloading the Server Packs, you need to download the Server Pack that is for your use-case.

#### Use-Cases

**Curseforge Installer**: Use the Curseforge Installer SRV pack if and ONLY if you are installing the Server Pack with the Curseforge installer (which is typical if you are using a host or self-hosting with a Pterodactyl instance running a Curseforge Egg)

**Standalone**: Use the Standalone SRV pack if you are using this for anything else.

The Standalone pack is a good fallback if you are unsure which is the proper use-case for you.

To be able to play this as a skyblock (which is the intended play-style), please follow the following:

### Skyblock Builder Method (This is the Intended Way)

*   Install the modpack.
*   If on a Singleplayer / LAN playthrough:
    *   Create a world with the generator of `Skyblock`.
*   If on a Server:
    *   Delete the `world` folder. (Make a backup first!)
    *   In the `server.properties` file:
    *   Change the line `level-type=minecraft\:normal` to `level-type=skyblockbuilder\:skyblock`
    *   Restart the server.

### Garden of Glass Method

*   Install the modpack.
*   If on a Singleplayer / LAN playthrough:
    *   Create a world with the generator of `Garden of Glass`.
*   If on a Server:
    *   Delete the `world` folder. (Make a backup first!)
    *   In the `server.properties` file:
    *   Change the line `level-type=minecraft\:normal` to `level-type=gardenofglass\:gardenofglass`
    *   Restart the server.

### **NOTE: If you are making this as a server, make sure to have a way for players to get a new island after joining. This is because by default, when a player joins for the first time, they will be sent to the Spawn island _which looks like a normal player island_. So, unless you want all players to play on one island (the Spawn island), then do this.**
